title: Canonical queue baseline and append-only updates must be reconstructed together
memory_recovered: Operational state is reconstructed from a dated canonical baseline plus later append-only, team-owned queue updates and evidence. Absence from operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json does not prove that a later item, artifact, decision, trigger attempt, or completion never existed.
reconstruction_order: read operations/CURRENT_STATE.json for boundaries and team ownership
read operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as the canonical baseline snapshot
apply later operations/queue-updates records in timestamp and commit order without overwriting prior events
cross-check referenced evidence files and commits
read operations/TRIGGER_REQUESTS.json and automation acknowledgements separately because a trigger request or schedule update is not peer-run evidence
retain conflicts as linked observations until a canonical merge explicitly resolves them

observed: operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is dated 2026-07-11 while later continuity queue-update commits exist on 2026-07-13
M-010 explicitly states that it is an append-only team-owned update and that ACTIVE_QUEUE.json was not overwritten
CURRENT_STATE.json requires teams to update only owned records and leave evidence plus peer-trigger requests
inferred: reading ACTIVE_QUEUE.json alone can produce false negatives about newer work
the effective operational queue is event-sourced until a verified canonical compaction occurs
proposed: add a deterministic queue-state materializer that validates ownership, timestamps, references, and conflict retention
publish a canonical compacted queue only through an explicit merge record that cites every consumed update
superseded: treating ACTIVE_QUEUE.json as a complete real-time account of all later team work
treating a reschedule acknowledgement or trigger request as proof that a peer run executed
unresolved: which existing queue updates have already been incorporated into a canonical successor snapshot
whether all team update filenames and timestamps satisfy one enforceable schema
whether the continuity index has incorporated CM-0013 through CM-0017

contradictions_resolved: A queue item can be absent from the canonical baseline and still be a valid later event; snapshot absence and event nonexistence are different claims.
A team can truthfully report a trigger attempt while the peer remains unexecuted; request evidence and execution evidence are different layers.
Avoiding shared-file overwrite does not mean avoiding shared state; append-only updates preserve coordination while preventing cross-team clobbering.
acceptance_test: A future state reader must not declare a later team item nonexistent solely because it is absent from ACTIVE_QUEUE.json; it must inspect and reconcile later team-owned queue updates and referenced evidence.
next_action: Implement a deterministic queue-state materializer and schema validator, then use its output to prepare a provenance-preserving canonical queue compaction without deleting or rewriting prior updates.
